title: Podman fails to push layer annotations on first attempt
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman push` on an image with layer annotations, the layer annotations aren't pushed to the remote registry unless the layer is already present. As a result, pushing the image a *second* time results in the layer annotations being uploaded.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Generate an image that has layer annotations. To test this, I used [build-chunked-oci](https://coreos.github.io/rpm-ostree/build-chunked-oci/) to rechunk `quay.io/fedora/fedora-iot:43`, producing an image (which I named `iot-rechunk-test`) with `"ostree.components"` layer annotations on all but the first layer.
2. Run `skopeo inspect containers-storage:iot-rechunk-test:latest` to confirm that the local image has layer annotations as expected.
3. Run `podman push` to push `iot-rechunk-test:latest` to ghcr.io.
4. Run `skopeo inspect docker://ghcr.io/[username]/iot-rechunk-test:latest` and observe that the remote copy of the image is missing layer annotations.
5. Run the same `podman push` command a second time.
6. Run the same `skopeo inspect docker://` command a second time and observe that the layer annotations are now present.

### Describe the results you received

Layer annotations are missing after pushing if the layer wasn't already present in the remote registry.

### Describe the results you expected

`podman push` should consistently preserve layer annotations.

### podman version output

```yaml
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.7.1
API Version:   5.7.1
Go Version:    go1.25.4 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:    f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
Built:         Tue Dec  9 19:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I was able to reproduce this in two environments:

* On my own computer (running secureblue, Kinoite variant, 2025-12-17 build) with rootless podman;
* On an Ubuntu 24.04 GitHub Actions runner using a container image to get up-to-date versions of rpm-ostree, podman, and skopeo. This used rootless podman on the host and rootful podman inside the container image. You can see the output of a test run of this workflow here (note, the `blue-build/rpm-ostree-container` image used here is basically just `quay.io/podman/stable` with `rpm-ostree` and `skopeo` preinstalled for convenience): https://github.com/HastD/scratchwork/actions/runs/20320059970/job/58373535963

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

sorry for the mess above...

This happens to also cause weird interactions with  `--sign-by-sigstore` to fix issues like: https://github.com/ublue-os/main/issues/643.


Reproducible with something like:

```
podman push quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc:latest ghcr.io/mytest/repo:tag --sign-by-sigstore sigstore-param-file.yaml
```

the image is signed and has no annotations and to workaround this bug to get the layer annotations, we do the same podman push again which invalidates **all** the layers (because we signed an image without the annotations?)

`sigstore-param-file.yaml`

```
privateKeyFile: cosign.key
privateKeyPassphraseFile: /dev/null
rekorURL: https://rekor.sigstore.dev
```

`podman version 5.8.2`
